perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be subject to and governed by the laws of the State of Colorado, excluding any conflicts-of-law rule or principle that might refer the construction or interpretation of this Agreement to the laws of another state. Each Party hereby submits to the jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in the State of Colorado and to venue in Denver, Colorado.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be subject to and governed by the laws of the State of Colorado, excluding any conflicts-of-law rule or principle that might refer the construction or interpretation of this Agreement to the laws of another state. Each Party hereby submits to the jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in the State of Colorado and to venue in Denver, Colorado. This agreement does not cover wage claims, which are subject to the jurisdiction where the employee performs the majority of their work.
perturbation.explanation: Adding a clause that subordinates the choice of law to the location of employee work for wage claims introduces a contradiction, as the original agreement establishes Colorado law as governing. This would confuse which jurisdiction's laws applies in wage disputes.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Colorado Wage Claim Act establishes specific procedures and remedies for wage claims within Colorado. Adding a clause prioritizing the employee's work location over Colorado law undermines the Act's intended scope and protections for employees working in Colorado, creating ambiguity about which state's wage laws apply.
perturbation.location: 2.1 Choice of Law; Submission to Jurisdiction.

perturbation.justification: The original agreement specifies Colorado law governs, but the changed text introduces an exception for wage claims, subjecting them to the jurisdiction where the employee works. The Law Explanation states this undermines the Colorado Wage Claim Act's scope and protections for employees working in Colorado, thus creating ambiguity about which state's wage laws apply. The missing snippets don't negate that the alteration introduces conflicting guidance regarding governing law, which weakens the primary clause.

perturbation.original_text: No Party shall have the right to assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the consent of the other Parties hereto; provided, however, that either party hereto may make a collateral assignment of this Agreement solely to secure working capital financing for such party.
perturbation.changed_text: No Party shall have the right to assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the consent of the other Parties hereto; provided, however, that either party hereto may make a collateral assignment of this Agreement solely to secure working capital financing for such party. This agreement may be freely assigned to any entity owned or controlled by a director of either party.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence conflicts with the initial clause. The original text requires consent for assignment, but the new sentence permits assignment to entities controlled by directors without explicit consent, making the original restriction unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law Principles Regarding Assignment
perturbation.law_explanation: General contract law, as reflected in the Restatement (Second) of Contracts, emphasizes clear agreement on assignment rights. By adding a clause that allows assignment to specific entities without consent, the modified text creates an ambiguity that undermines the enforceability of the initial restriction on assignment, potentially causing disputes over whether proper consent was obtained.

perturbation.justification: The original text requires consent for any assignment of rights, whereas the changed text creates an exception for assignments to entities controlled by directors without obtaining the consent of the other party. This modification introduces an ambiguity, undermining the initial restriction on assignment and thus creating a legal contradiction as described by the Perturbation and Law Explanations. The scraped law snippets are unhelpful.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified from time to time only by the written agreement of all the Parties hereto. Each such instrument shall be reduced to writing and shall be designated on its face an "Amendment" or an "Addendum" to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified from time to time only by the written agreement of all the Parties hereto. Each such instrument shall be reduced to writing and shall be designated on its face an "Amendment" or an "Addendum" to this Agreement. However, the reimbursement rate in Section 1.2 can be changed at any time by the EmployeeCo's CFO with notification to the Operating Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates written agreement for all amendments. The added sentence allows the EmployeeCo's CFO to unilaterally alter the reimbursement rate, contradicting the initial requirement for mutual agreement, and creates an exception that undermines the clause's enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law Principles Regarding Amendments
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-209, while technically dealing with sales of goods, reflects the general principle that contract modifications require mutual assent. Allowing one party's CFO to unilaterally change the reimbursement rate directly contradicts the initial clause requiring written agreement from all parties for any amendments, leading to a legal contradiction in amendment procedures.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-209. Modification, Rescission and Waiver. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-209. Modification, Rescission and Waiver. (1) An agreement modifying a contract within this Article needs no consideration to be binding. (2) A signed agreement which excludes modification or rescission except by a signed writing cannot be otherwise modified or rescinded, but except as between merchants such a requirement on a form supplied by the merchant must be separately signed by the other party. (3) The requirements of the statute of frauds section of this Article (Section 2-201 ) must be satisfied if the contract as modified is within its provisions. (4)Although an attempt at modification or rescission does not satisfy the requirements of subsection (2) or (3) it can operate as a waiver. (5) A party who has made a waiver affecting an executory portion of the contract may retract the waiver by reasonable notification received by the other party that strict performance will be required of any term waived, unless the retraction would be unjust in view of a material change of position in reliance on the waiver. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a clear contradiction. The original clause requires written agreement from all parties for any amendments. The added sentence allows the EmployeeCo's CFO to unilaterally alter the reimbursement rate, which directly contradicts the initial requirement for mutual agreement. UCC § 2-209(2), as presented in Scraped Law Snippet 1, states that 'A signed agreement which excludes modification or rescission except by a signed writing cannot be otherwise modified or rescinded'. The changed text violates this principle by allowing a modification (the reimbursement rate) without a signed writing from all parties, undermining the clause's enforceability and creating a Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction. The fact that the UCC deals with the sale of goods, as stated in the Law Explanation, does not negate the broader principle of mutual assent in contract modifications.
